items.topic_thesis: To found trust without building a cult, leaders must transgress, recognize, release, and submit.
items.angle: Do not summarize the news; diagnose the pattern underneath it.
items.hook: Most people are misreading what this story is actually about. to found trust without building a cult, leaders must transgress, recognize, release, and submit.

items.topic_thesis: Living law has to submit itself to the same anti-scapegoat logic it imposes on everyone else.
items.angle: The interpretation is the point, not the quote itself.
items.hook: A note landed in my inbox that names a pattern I keep seeing. living law has to submit itself to the same anti-scapegoat logic it imposes on everyone else.

items.topic_thesis: The faster knowledge changes, the more an organization needs a stable myth layer.
items.angle: Name the concept, explain it plainly, and show why it matters now.
items.hook: The mistake leaders keep making is thinking the faster knowledge changes, the more an organization needs a stable myth layer.

items.topic_thesis: Apollo and Dionysus are complements, not substitutes: one optimizes the known system, the other governs death and rebirth.
items.angle: Make the pattern recognizable fast and show what drives it.
items.hook: You can tell a team is drifting into theater when apollo and dionysus are complements, not substitutes: one optimizes the known system, the other governs death and rebirth.

items.topic_thesis: The Weekly Crossing is a practical recurring ritual for metabolizing drift before it hardens.
items.angle: Start with a recurring problem and end with a usable move.
items.hook: If your team keeps repeating the same failure, stop adding process. the weekly crossing is a practical recurring ritual for metabolizing drift before it hardens.
